relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Abram came up from Egypt with Sarai, Lot, and all he had, returning toward the land of promise.
Abram carried great wealth, heavy with livestock, silver, and gold.
He journeyed back toward Bethel, returning to the place where his tent had first stood.
There, at the first altar, Abram again called on the name of the Eternal.
Lot too had grown in possessions, with flocks, herds, and tents.
The land could not carry both households together; abundance had become pressure.
Strife arose among the herders while other peoples still dwelled in the land.
Abram chose peace over possession, reminding Lot that they were kin.
He opened space and gave Lot first choice, releasing control instead of fighting for advantage.
Lot lifted his eyes and chose what looked abundant, a watered plain beautiful like Eden and Egypt, though shadow already waited near Sodom.
Lot chose the plain and journeyed east, and the two households separated.
Abram remained in Canaan, while Lot moved toward the cities of the plain and pitched near Sodom.
Sodom was already marked by harm and offense before the Eternal.
After Lot separated, the Eternal told Abram to lift his eyes too, not to grasp, but to receive vision.
All the land Abram saw was given into promise for him and his seed.
His seed would be like dust of the earth, uncountable and woven into the ground itself.
Abram was told to rise and walk the promise, to let his body move through what the Divine had spoken.
He settled by the oaks of Mamre in Hebron and built another altar, rooting the journey in worship.
